Análisis de factores demográficos, clínicos y de estilo de vida en la predicción del riesgo de diabetes

Samuel Arandia, Juan David Castañeda, Juan Felipe Rojas

Importamos las bases de datos

library(dplyr)
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)


setwd('.')

questionary <- read.csv("data/questionnaire.csv")[ c("SEQN","DIQ010", "SMQ020", "PAQ650") ]

labs <- read.csv("data/labs.csv")[ c("SEQN", "LBXGH", "LBXTC", "LBXIN")]

examination <- read.csv("data/examination.csv")[ c("SEQN", "BPXSY1", "BMXWT", "BMXHT", "BMXBMI")]

demographic <- read.csv("data/demographic.csv") [c("SEQN", "RIAGENDR", "RIDRETH3", "DMDEDUC2", "RIDAGEYR")]

dieta <- read.csv("data/diet.csv")[ c( "SEQN", "DR1TCARB", "DR1TFIBE") ]

Ahora hacemos merge a las bases de datos para obtener una única grande

db1 <- merge(questionary, labs, by = "SEQN", all = FALSE)

db2 <- merge(examination, demographic, by = "SEQN", all = FALSE)

db3 <- merge(db1, db2, by = "SEQN", all = FALSE)


db <- merge(db3, dieta, by = "SEQN", all = FALSE)

Renombramos las variables de acuerdo al diccionario de datos por nombres más entendibles

  • SEQN: Id
  • DIQ010: Diabetes_diagnostized
  • SMQ020: Smoked_100_cigarrette
  • PAQ650: Sport
  • LBXGH: Glycohemoglobin
  • LBXTC: Cholesterol
  • LBXIN: Insulina
  • BPXSY1: Blood_pressure
  • BMXWT: Weight
  • BMXHT: Height
  • BMXBMI: BMI
  • RIAGENDR: Gender
  • RIDRETH3: Etnicity
  • DMDEDUC2: Education
  • RIDAGEYR: Age
  • DR1TCARB: Carbohydrate
  • DR1TFIBE: Fiber

Consideraremos a los adultos apartir de la mediana edad, pues son la población más afectada por esta enferemedad y de la que se encuentra mejor documentada

db <- db[(db$RIDAGEYR >= 45),]

head(db)
##    SEQN DIQ010 SMQ020 PAQ650 LBXGH LBXTC LBXIN BPXSY1 BMXWT BMXHT BMXBMI
## 1 73557      1      1      2  13.9   167    NA    122  78.3 171.3   26.7
## 2 73558      1      1      2   9.1   170    NA    156  89.5 176.8   28.6
## 3 73559      1      1      2   8.9   126  5.83    140  88.9 175.3   28.9
## 5 73561      2      2      2   4.9   201  6.12    136  52.0 162.4   19.7
## 6 73562      2      1      2   5.5   226    NA    160 105.0 158.7   41.7
## 8 73564      2      2      2   5.5   168 14.91    118  93.4 161.8   35.7
##   RIAGENDR RIDRETH3 DMDEDUC2 RIDAGEYR DR1TCARB DR1TFIBE
## 1        1        4        3       69   239.59     10.8
## 2        1        3        3       54   423.78     16.7
## 3        1        3        4       72   224.39      9.9
## 5        2        3        5       73   178.20     12.3
## 6        1        1        4       56   189.59     22.6
## 8        2        3        5       61   300.16     24.8
names(db) <- c("Id", "Diabetes_diagnostized", "Smoked_100_cigarrette", "Sport",
               "Glycohemoglobin", "Cholesterol", "Insulina", "Blood_pressure", 
               "Weight", "Height", "BMI", "Gender", "Etnicity", "Education", 
               "Age", "Carbohydrate", "Fiber")

head(db)
##      Id Diabetes_diagnostized Smoked_100_cigarrette Sport Glycohemoglobin
## 1 73557                     1                     1     2            13.9
## 2 73558                     1                     1     2             9.1
## 3 73559                     1                     1     2             8.9
## 5 73561                     2                     2     2             4.9
## 6 73562                     2                     1     2             5.5
## 8 73564                     2                     2     2             5.5
##   Cholesterol Insulina Blood_pressure Weight Height  BMI Gender Etnicity
## 1         167       NA            122   78.3  171.3 26.7      1        4
## 2         170       NA            156   89.5  176.8 28.6      1        3
## 3         126     5.83            140   88.9  175.3 28.9      1        3
## 5         201     6.12            136   52.0  162.4 19.7      2        3
## 6         226       NA            160  105.0  158.7 41.7      1        1
## 8         168    14.91            118   93.4  161.8 35.7      2        3
##   Education Age Carbohydrate Fiber
## 1         3  69       239.59  10.8
## 2         3  54       423.78  16.7
## 3         4  72       224.39   9.9
## 5         5  73       178.20  12.3
## 6         4  56       189.59  22.6
## 8         5  61       300.16  24.8

Reescribiendo variables categoricas

db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    Gender = factor(Gender, 
                    levels = c(1,2), 
                    labels = c("Male", "Female")
   ), 
    
    Education = factor(Education,
                    levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9),
                    labels = c("Less than 9th grade", 
                               "9th-11th grade", 
                               "High school graduate/GED", 
                               "Some college or AA degree", 
                               "College graduate or above",
                               "Refused",
                               "Don't Know")
    ), 
    Etnicity = factor(Diabetes_diagnostized, 
                    levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7), 
                    labels = c("Mexican American", "Other Hispanic", "Non-Hispanic White", 
                               "Non-Hispanic Black", "Non-Hispanic Asian", 
                               "Other Race - Including Multi-Racial")), 
    
    Diabetes_diagnostized = factor(Diabetes_diagnostized, 
                    levels = c(1, 2, 3, 7, 9), 
                    labels = c("Yes", "No", "Borderline", "Refused", "Don't know")),

    Smoked_100_cigarrette = factor(Smoked_100_cigarrette, 
                    levels = c(1, 2, 7, 9), 
                    labels = c("Yes", "No", "Refused", "Don't know")),
    

    Sport = factor(Sport, 
                    levels = c(1, 2, 7, 9), 
                    labels = c("Yes", "No", "Refused", "Don't know"))
  )

Variables categoricas

Tablas de frecuencia:

categorical_data <- db[c("Diabetes_diagnostized", "Smoked_100_cigarrette", "Sport", "Gender", 
                         "Etnicity")]
summary(categorical_data)
##  Diabetes_diagnostized Smoked_100_cigarrette        Sport         Gender    
##  Yes       : 620       Yes       :1543       Yes       : 415   Male  :1517  
##  No        :2419       No        :1629       No        :2759   Female:1657  
##  Borderline: 133       Refused   :   0       Refused   :   0                
##  Refused   :   0       Don't know:   2       Don't know:   0                
##  Don't know:   2                                                            
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##                                 Etnicity   
##  Mexican American                   : 620  
##  Other Hispanic                     :2419  
##  Non-Hispanic White                 : 133  
##  Non-Hispanic Black                 :   0  
##  Non-Hispanic Asian                 :   0  
##  Other Race - Including Multi-Racial:   0  
##  NA's                               :   2

Histogramas

ggplot(db, aes(x=Diabetes_diagnostized , fill=Diabetes_diagnostized )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none")

ggplot(db, aes(x=Smoked_100_cigarrette , fill=Smoked_100_cigarrette )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none")

ggplot(db, aes(x=Sport , fill=Sport )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none")

ggplot(db, aes(x=Gender , fill=Gender )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none")

ggplot(db, aes(x=Etnicity , fill=Etnicity )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none")+ 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggplot(db, aes(x=Education, fill=Education )) + 
  geom_bar( ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position="none") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Variables númericas

Tabla de medidas de tendencia central y posición

Numeric_variables <- db[c("Age", "Glycohemoglobin", "Cholesterol", "Insulina", "Blood_pressure", "Weight",
                          "Height", "BMI", "Carbohydrate", "Fiber")]

summary(Numeric_variables)
##       Age        Glycohemoglobin   Cholesterol     Insulina     
##  Min.   :45.00   Min.   : 3.500   Min.   : 82   Min.   :  0.14  
##  1st Qu.:53.00   1st Qu.: 5.400   1st Qu.:163   1st Qu.:  5.83  
##  Median :61.00   Median : 5.700   Median :191   Median :  9.30  
##  Mean   :62.02   Mean   : 6.006   Mean   :193   Mean   : 13.80  
##  3rd Qu.:70.00   3rd Qu.: 6.100   3rd Qu.:219   3rd Qu.: 14.88  
##  Max.   :80.00   Max.   :17.500   Max.   :813   Max.   :682.48  
##                  NA's   :109      NA's   :144   NA's   :1721    
##  Blood_pressure      Weight          Height           BMI       
##  Min.   : 66.0   Min.   : 32.3   Min.   :135.4   Min.   :14.20  
##  1st Qu.:116.0   1st Qu.: 66.6   1st Qu.:158.5   1st Qu.:24.70

##  Mean   :129.2   Mean   : 81.0   Mean   :166.0   Mean   :29.31  
##  3rd Qu.:140.0   3rd Qu.: 91.9   3rd Qu.:173.2   3rd Qu.:32.60  
##  Max.   :228.0   Max.   :207.2   Max.   :202.6   Max.   :82.90  
##  NA's   :302     NA's   :40      NA's   :44      NA's   :52     
##   Carbohydrate         Fiber       
##  Min.   :  12.29   Min.   :  0.00  
##  1st Qu.: 156.31   1st Qu.:  9.70  
##  Median : 214.69   Median : 14.90  
##  Mean   : 234.71   Mean   : 17.08  
##  3rd Qu.: 292.91   3rd Qu.: 21.90  
##  Max.   :1011.84   Max.   :107.00  
##  NA's   :341       NA's   :341

Tabla de medidas de tendencia central con coeficiente de variacion:

medidas_descriptivas <- db %>%
  summarise(
    across(c(Age, Weight, Height, BMI, Blood_pressure, Cholesterol, 
             Glycohemoglobin, Insulina, Carbohydrate, Fiber),
           list(
             Media = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
             Mediana = ~median(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
             Varianza = ~var(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
             Desv_Estandar = ~sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
             Coef_Variacion = ~(sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
             Q1 = ~quantile(.x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
             Q3 = ~quantile(.x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
           ),
           .names = "{.col}__{.fn}" 
    )
  )

tabla_formateada <- medidas_descriptivas %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), 
               names_to = c("Variable", "Medida"), 
               names_sep = "__") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Medida, values_from = value)

kable(tabla_formateada, digits = 2, align = "c", 
      caption = "Tabla 1. Medidas de Tendencia Central, Variabilidad (con CV %) y Posición")
Tabla 1. Medidas de Tendencia Central, Variabilidad (con CV %) y Posición
Variable Media Mediana Varianza Desv_Estandar Coef_Variacion Q1 Q3
Age 62.02 61.00 115.14 10.73 17.30 53.00 70.00
Weight 81.00 78.30 434.92 20.85 25.75 66.60 91.90
Height 166.01 165.70 103.76 10.19 6.14 158.50 173.20
BMI 29.31 28.10 46.97 6.85 23.38 24.70 32.60
Blood_pressure 129.16 126.00 376.93 19.41 15.03 116.00 140.00
Cholesterol 192.99 191.00 1870.49 43.25 22.41 163.00 219.00
Glycohemoglobin 6.01 5.70 1.42 1.19 19.84 5.40 6.10
Insulina 13.80 9.30 568.76 23.85 172.86 5.83 14.88
Carbohydrate 234.71 214.69 13137.48 114.62 48.83 156.31 292.91
Fiber 17.08 14.90 112.70 10.62 62.17 9.70 21.90

Age

p1 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "skyblue", bins = 20) +
  ggtitle("Histogram")

p2 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Density (KDE)")

p3 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
  ggtitle("Boxplot")

p4 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = "", y = Age)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "purple", alpha = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Violin Plot")

(p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)

Glycohemoglobin

p1 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Glycohemoglobin )) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "skyblue", bins = 20) +
  ggtitle("Histogram")

p2 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Glycohemoglobin )) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Density (KDE)")

p3 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = Glycohemoglobin )) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
  ggtitle("Boxplot")

p4 <- ggplot(db, aes(x = "", y = Glycohemoglobin )) +
  geom_violin(fill = "purple", alpha = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Violin Plot")

(p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)

Graficos bivariados

IMC basado en el diagnóstico de diabetes

grafica_cajas <- ggplot(db %>% filter(!is.na(Diabetes_diagnostized)), 
       aes(x = Diabetes_diagnostized, y = BMI, fill = Diabetes_diagnostized)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribución del Índice de Masa Corporal (IMC) según Diagnóstico",
       x = "Diagnóstico de Diabetes",
       y = "Índice de Masa Corporal (BMI)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(grafica_cajas)

Genero vs Diagnostico de Diabetes

tabla_cruzada <- db %>%
  filter(!is.na(Gender) & !is.na(Diabetes_diagnostized)) %>%
  count(Gender, Diabetes_diagnostized) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Diabetes_diagnostized, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

kable(tabla_cruzada, align = "c", 
      caption = "Tabla 2. Frecuencias absolutas: Género vs Diagnóstico de Diabetes")
Tabla 2. Frecuencias absolutas: Género vs Diagnóstico de Diabetes
Gender Yes No Borderline Don’t know
Male 310 1134 73 0
Female 310 1285 60 2

Proporcion del diagnostico de diabetes segun genero

grafica_barras <- ggplot(db %>% filter(!is.na(Gender) & !is.na(Diabetes_diagnostized)), 
       aes(x = Gender, fill = Diabetes_diagnostized)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Proporción del Diagnóstico de Diabetes según Género",
       x = "Género",
       y = "Porcentaje dentro del grupo",
       fill = "Diagnóstico")

print(grafica_barras)

Edad vs Diagnostico

grafica_densidad <- ggplot(db %>% filter(!is.na(Diabetes_diagnostized) & Diabetes_diagnostized %in% c("Yes", "No")), 
       aes(x = Age, fill = Diabetes_diagnostized)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribución Poblacional de la Edad según Diagnóstico Confirmado",
       x = "Edad (años)",
       y = "Densidad de Probabilidad",
       fill = "Diagnóstico")

print(grafica_densidad)

Actividad Fisica vs Insulina

grafica_deporte <- ggplot(db %>% filter(!is.na(Sport) & !is.na(Insulina)), 
       aes(x = Sport, y = Insulina, fill = Sport)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, color = "black", outlier.color = "red", outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Hacemos un zoom limitando el eje Y para que los valores atípicos extremos no aplasten las cajas
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 150)) + 
  labs(title = "Distribución de Insulina según Práctica de Actividad Física Vigorosa",
       x = "¿Realiza actividad física vigorosa?",
       y = "Niveles de Insulina",
       fill = "Actividad Física")
print(grafica_deporte)

grafica_violin <- ggplot(db %>% filter(!is.na(Diabetes_diagnostized) & !is.na(Glycohemoglobin) & Diabetes_diagnostized %in% c("Yes", "No", "Borderline")), 
       aes(x = Diabetes_diagnostized, y = Glycohemoglobin, fill = Diabetes_diagnostized)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, color = "black", alpha = 0.8, outlier.size = 1) +
  # Línea de referencia clínica (Aprox 6.5% es el umbral diagnóstico de diabetes)
  geom_hline(yintercept = 6.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribución y Densidad de Hemoglobina Glicosilada por Diagnóstico",
       subtitle = "Línea roja punteada indica el umbral clínico referencial (6.5%)",
       x = "Diagnóstico Médico Previo",
       y = "Hemoglobina Glicosilada (%)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(grafica_violin)

tabla_deporte <- db %>%
  filter(Sport %in% c("Yes", "No") & Diabetes_diagnostized %in% c("Yes", "No", "Borderline")) %>%
  count(Sport, Diabetes_diagnostized) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Diabetes_diagnostized, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

library(kableExtra)
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'kableExtra'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     group_rows
kable(tabla_deporte, align = "c", 
      caption = "Tabla 3. Frecuencias absolutas: Actividad Física vs Diagnóstico") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "bordered", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE, 
                font_size = 14) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Diagnóstico de Diabetes" = 3)) %>% # Le agrega un súper título a las columnas
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = "#2c3e50") # Pone el encabezado oscuro y elegante
Tabla 3. Frecuencias absolutas: Actividad Física vs Diagnóstico
Diagnóstico de Diabetes
Sport Yes No Borderline
Yes 40 361 14
No 580 2058 119 
print(tabla_deporte)
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   Sport   Yes    No Borderline
##   <fct> <int> <int>      <int>
## 1 Yes      40   361         14
## 2 No      580  2058        119
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra) 


freq = table(db$Diabetes_diagnostized)
prop = prop.table(freq) * 100

tabla = data.frame(
  Categoría = names(freq),
  Frecuencia = as.vector(freq),
  Porcentaje = paste0(round(as.vector(prop), 2), "%")
)


kable(tabla,
      align = "c",
      caption = "Distribución de Frecuencias: Diagnóstico de Diabetes") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "bordered", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE, 
                font_size = 14) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = "#2c3e50")
Distribución de Frecuencias: Diagnóstico de Diabetes
Categoría Frecuencia Porcentaje
Yes 620 19.53%
No 2419 76.21%
Borderline 133 4.19%
Refused 0 0%
Don’t know 2 0.06% 
library(treemapify)
datos_etnia <- db %>%
  filter(!is.na(Etnicity)) %>%
  count(Etnicity) %>%
  mutate(Porcentaje = n / sum(n) * 100,
         Etiqueta = paste0(Etnicity, "\n", n, " hab.\n(", round(Porcentaje, 1), "%)"))

grafica_treemap <- ggplot(datos_etnia, aes(area = n, fill = Etnicity, label = Etiqueta)) +
  geom_treemap(color = "white", size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_treemap_text(colour = "white", place = "centre", grow = FALSE, reflow = TRUE, fontface = "bold", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") + # Paleta de colores profesional
  labs(title = "Composición Étnica de la Población de Estudio",
       subtitle = "Distribución proporcional basada en la muestra filtrada (> 45 años)",
       fill = "Etnicidad") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 

print(grafica_treemap)
